original_text: All birds can fly. A sparrow is a bird. Can the sparrow fly?

samples.text: All birds can fly. A sparrow is a bird. Can the sparrow fly?
samples.notes: Original sample. No semantic transformation is applied.

samples.text: Every bird is capable of flight. A sparrow belongs to the bird category. Is the sparrow able to fly?
samples.notes: The wording changes, but the universal rule and taxonomic relation remain unchanged.

samples.text: All avians can fly. A sparrow is an avian. Can the sparrow fly?
samples.notes: The word 'bird' is replaced by 'avian' while preserving the same taxonomic inference.

samples.text: All birds can fly. Apples are usually red. A sparrow is a bird. Can the sparrow fly?
samples.notes: The distractor sentence is irrelevant and does not change the inference structure.

samples.text: Most birds can fly. A sparrow is a bird. Can the sparrow fly?
samples.notes: Changing 'all' to 'most' weakens the universal rule and no longer guarantees the conclusion.

samples.text: All birds can fly. A bat is a mammal. Can the bat fly?
samples.notes: The entity changes from a bird to a mammal, so the original taxonomic inference no longer applies.
